title: Fast-S: Single tube amplification and PCR barcoding of SARS-CoV-2 S gene  for Nanopore sequencing
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"emg51","text":"Most of the defining mutations of the severe acute respiratory syndrome coronavirus-2 (SARS-CoV-2) variants of concern (VOCs) have been identified in the S gene sequence. For this reason, S-based lineage assignment is possible using the current nomenclature system. We have developed a protocol for overlapping amplification of the S gene sequence using previously reported primer sequences (V3 primers of ARTIC Network) in combination with a PCR barcoding approach of the samples for Nanopore sequencing platforms. This protocol allows a fast and cost-effective screening of COVID-19 positive samples for lineage/clade assignment and mutational surveillance of the spike gene.","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ff7n8","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"e5q30","text":"Follow standard health and safety guidelines when manipulating COVID-19 patient samples. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

sequences","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":17}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"e0e62","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bg1g3","text":"The primers used in this protocol are from the V3 scheme from the ARTIC Network group (https://github.com/artic-network/primer-schemes/blob/master/nCoV-2019/V3/nCoV-2019.tsv) for SARS-CoV-2 whole genome sequencing. The selection of primers was based in the Oxford Nanopore protocol \"PCR tiling of SARS-CoV-2 spike protein gene with rapid barcoding and Spike Seq RT PCR Expansion (SQK-RBK110.96 and EXP-SRT001), Version: SRT-9128_v110_revB_14Jul2021\" (https://nanoporetech.com/). These primers span the S gene region of the SARS-CoV-2 genome from approximately the position 21076 to 26315 of the Wuhan reference (WIV04) and have the ONT universal sequence tags for compatibility with the Ligation sequencing amplicons - PCR barcoding (SQK-LSK109 with EXP-PBC096) protocol (https://nanoporetech.com/).","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":87,"length":87},{"key":1,"offset":451,"length":27},{"key":2,"offset":772,"length":26}],"data":{}},{"key":"cntur","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"qfe4","text":"Forward: steps: Keeping the SARS-CoV-2 extracted RNA samples on ice all the time and spin down the tubes.

Set up the RT-PCR reaction tubes in a clean pre-PCR cabinet by adding 2 μL of LunaScript® RT SuperMix to each PCR tube. Include a RT-PCR negative control by replacing RNA sample with nuclease free water. Add negative and positive controls for results validation.
Add 8 uL of sample to the tube containing the LunaScript® RT SuperMix and mix gently. The final volume of the reaction is 10 μL.

Incubate in a thermal cycler using the the following instructions: Step Temperature (ºC) Time Cycles 1 Primer annealing 25 2 min 1 2 cDNA Synthesis 55 10 min 3 Heat inactivation 95 1 min 4 Hold 10 - Table 2: SARS-CoV-2 RT-PCR thermal profile.
Place PCR tubes with caps in a 96 sample rack for pool A and pool B amplification step.

Label a set of PCR tube caps for amplification using the pool A and pool B according to the Oxford Nanopore PCR Barcoding Expansion 1-96 (stock 10 μM). You will need two sets of PCR tubes with the same barcode identification. PCR strips caps are more easy to handle. - 1A/1B 2A/2B 3A/3B 4A/4B 5A/5B 6A/6B 7A/7B 8A/8B 9A/9B 10A/10B 11A/11B 12A/12B 1 A BC01 BC02 BC03 BC04 BC05 BC06 BC07 BC08 BC09 BC10 BC11 BC12 2 B BC13 BC14 BC15 BC16 BC17 BC18 BC19 BC20 BC21 BC22 BC23 BC24 3 C BC25 BC26 BC27 BC28 BC29 BC30 BC31 BC32 BC33 BC34 BC35 BC36 4 D BC37 BC38 BC39 BC40 BC41 BC42 BC43 BC44 BC45 BC46 BC47 BC48 5 E BC49 BC50 BC51 BC52 BC53 BC54 BC55 BC56 BC57 BC58 BC59 BC60 6 F BC61 BC62 BC63 BC64 BC65 BC66 BC67 BC68 BC69 BC70 BC71 BC72 7 G BC73 BC74 BC75 BC76 BC77 BC78 BC79 BC80 BC81 BC82 BC83 BC84 8 H BC85 BC86 BC87 BC88 BC89 BC90 BC91 BC92 BC93 BC94 BC95 BC96 Table 1: Oxford Nanopore PCR Barcoding Expansion 1-96 tube labeling for pool A and pool B amplification step

Spin down briefly the PCR Barcoding Expansion 1-96 tubes and open each carefully avoiding spray generation. Transfer 1 μL of each barcode using a multichannel pipette to the PCR tube cap and incubate 1hr at 37 ºC or at room temperature until the drop has dried. Optional: add 1 μL of a dilution of 1:10 of a dye, such as TriTrack DNA Loading Dye (6X) to visualize the drop in the PCR tube cap. Store in a dry place until use.

Set up the first round PCR reaction in a pre-PCR cabinet for primer pool A and primer pool B Reagent PCR master mix pool A PCR master mix pool B 1 Nuclease free water 400 μL 400 μL 2 Primer pool A 30 μM) 25 μL -- 3 Primer pool B (30 μM) -- 25 μL 4 LongAmp® Taq 2X Master Mix 625 μL 625 μL 5 Final volume 1050 μL 1050 μL Table 3: S gene PCR multiplex amplification master mixes for pool A and B.
Transfer 10,5 μL of the PCR Master mix Pool A to the PCR tube set A and 10,5 μLof the PCR Master mix Pool B to the PCR tube set B, respectively.

Using a multichannel pipette, transfer 2 μL of reversed transcribed product from the "Reverse Transcription" section to the corresponding Pool A and Pool B PCR strip tubes. Carry over the negative and positive controls. Store at -20 ºC the remaining reverse transcribed sample for further characterization, if needed.

Spin down briefly and replace the PCR strip caps with the PCR tube caps containing the immobilized Barcoding Expansion 1-96 barcodes as described in the "Barcode immobilization" section. Make sure to match the sample and barcode ID correctly for both pool A and B.

Incubate in the thermal cycler with the following program: Step Temperature (ºC) Time Cycles 1 Initial denaturation 94 30 s 1 2 Denaturation 94 15 s 20 3 Annealing and extension 63 3 m 4 Hold 10 -- -- Table 4: S gene PCR amplification using the LongAmp™ Taq 2X polymerase.

Remove the PCR strips off the thermal cycler and invert the tubes at least 10 times until the immobilized PCR barcodes in the PCR strip caps are dissolved in the PCR master mix from the amplification step. Spin down and return the tubes into the thermal cycler and proceed to the barcoding step: Step Temperature (ºC) Time Cycles 1 Initial denaturation 95 3 m 1 2 Denaturation 95 15 s 15 3 Annealing 62 15 s 4 Extension 65 50 s 5 Final extension 65 10 m 1 6 Hold 10 -- -- Table 5: S gene PCR barcoding of S gene amplified material using the amplification PCR master mix from the amplification step We use a 4X molar excess of barcodes for the PCR barcoding step.
Spin down the tubes and pool all samples in a 1.5 mL LoBind tube.

Add 0.5X volume of Ampure XP beads. Incubate 5 minutes in a rotator mixer. Spin down and rest the tubes in a magnetic rack for 5 minutes. Make sure AMPure XP beads have reached room temperature before use.
Discard the supernatant by aspiration, taking care not to disturb the pellet beads.
Wash the beads with Ethanol 70%. Repeat this step.
Let the pellet air dry for ~30 seconds and add 50 uL of nuclease free water. Incubate for 2 minutes at room temperature, spin down and place the PCR tubes in the magnetic rack for 5 minutes.
Recover the supernatant.
Prepare 1 µg of clean barcoded pool in 48 µL. Take the barcoded pool the repair and end-prep step or store the DNA at 4 ºC overnight or -20 ºC for long term storage.

Prepare the end prep mix as follows: Sample/Reagent Volume (μL) 1 NEBNext FFPE DNA Repair Mix 2 2 Ultra II End-prep enzyme mix 3 3 NEBNext FFPE DNA Repair Buffer 3.5 4 Ultra II End-prep reaction buffer 3.5 5 Clean barcoded pool (1 µg) 48 6 Final volume 60 Table 6: End prep reaction mix. (Optional) DNA CS can be added for internal control and to increase DNA in the final library.
Incubate the End-prep mix in the thermal cycler as follows: Step Temperature (ºC) Time 1 Enzymes incubation 20 5 min 2 Enzymes inactivation 65 5 min Table 7: End-prep thermal incubation

[Optional] Add 1X volume of AMPure XP beads to the reaction and incubate in the rotator mixer for 5 minutes. Spin down and rest the tubes in the magnetic rack for 5 minutes. Discard the supernatant by aspiration, taking care not to disturb the pellet beads. Let the pellet air dry for ~30 seconds and add 61 µL of nuclease free water and incubate at room temperature for 2 minutes.Spin down and rest the tubes in the magnetic rack for 5 minutes and recover the supernatant. Spin down and rest the tubes in the magnetic rack for 5 minutes and recover the supernatant. Take forward the end-prepped DNA into the adapter ligation step or store the sample at 4 ºC overnight

Prepare the following adapter ligation mix: Reagent Volume (μL) 1 Clean end-prepped DNA 60 2 Ligation Buffer (LNB) 25 3 NEBNext Quick T4 DNA Ligase 10 4 AMX adapter 5 5 Final volume 100 Table 8: Adapter (AMX) ligation mix.
Incubate the reaction for 10 minutes at room temperature. Do not incubate the reaction for longer than 10 minutes if the AMPure XP beads purification was omitted.
Spin down the tubes and add AMPure XP beads 0.4X volume. Incubate in the rotator mixer for 5 minutes at room temperature.
Spin down the tubes and rest the tubes in the magnet rack for 5 minutes. Pipette off the supernatant.
Add 200 µL of Short Fragment Buffer (SFB) and mix gently. Spin down and place the tube in the magnet rack for 5 minutes. Discard the supernatant.
Repeat the previous step.

Remove the tubes from the magnet and add 15 µL of Elution Buffer (EB). Flick the tube, spin down briefly and incubate the tube for 10 minutes at 37 ºC. The final library can be stored for up to 3 days at 4 ºC.
Quantify the final library using a fluorometric assay.
Use the Nanopore standard procedure for priming the FLO-MIN106D or FLO-FLG001 flow cells. https://community.nanoporetech.com/nanopore_learning/lessons/priming-and-loading-your-flow-cell

Once the flow cell is correctly primer proceed to load the library mixing the following: Sample/Reagent Volume (uL) 1 Sequencing Buffer (SQB) 37.5 2 Loading Beads (LB) 25.5 3 DNA library (~300 ng) 12 4 Final volume 75 Table 9: DNA library to load in a FLO-MIN106D. A B 1 Sequencing Buffer (SQB) 15 2 Loading Beads (LB) 10 3 DNA library (~200 ng) 5 4 Final Volume 30 Table 9: DNA library to load in a FLO-FLG001. We recommend to load initially 300 ng for the FLO-MIN106D and 200 ng for the FLO-FLG001 and monitor the pore occupancy over the first 20 minutes and reload if the pore occupancy is lower than 60%. Additionally, a refuel after 12 hours of the sequencing run is recommended. Note that the amount of DNA library is significantly higher than the amount recommended by Oxford Nanopore (20-50 fmol). If not sure, begin from this number and upscale accordingly to reach a satisfactory pore occupancy.

Use epi2me-labs/wf-artic V1 scheme for consensus generation wf-artic hedgehog for lineage set assignment using maximum ambiguity hedgehog Use president for S gene completeness president and samtools for average sequencing depth samtools Run the sequencing experiment until reaching at least 300X of average sequencing depth and or more than 90% of S gene completeness for optimal results.
creator: Cecilia Salazar
